name: PyCryptodome (Self-Contained Cryptographic Library)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-2/public domain); Heliodoro Telles Snell

performance.notes: C extension implementation; competitive with cryptography package

agent_readiness.notes: Self-contained Python crypto library — drop-in replacement for PyCrypto. from Crypto.Cipher import AES. from Crypto.Hash import SHA256. from Crypto.PublicKey import RSA. from Crypto.Signature import pkcs1_15. key = RSA.generate(2048). cipher = AES.new(key, AES.MODE_GCM). ciphertext, tag = cipher.encrypt_and_digest(data). AES-GCM, AES-CBC, ChaCha20-Poly1305, RSA, DSA, ECC, SHA2/SHA3, BLAKE2, Scrypt, PBKDF2. pycryptodomex (namespace Cryptodome) for no-conflict installs. PyCryptodome vs cryptography package: PyCryptodome has more algorithms, cryptography package is better maintained.

gotchas: PyCrypto namespace conflict — use pycryptodomex for Cryptodome namespace if PyCrypto is anywhere in deps
cryptography package (PyCA) is generally preferred for new code — better hazmat API separation
Some algorithms are legacy/insecure — easy to accidentally use DES or RC4
No high-level TLS/X.509 support (use cryptography package for that)
best_when: Replacing legacy PyCrypto code (drop-in compatible namespace)
When specific algorithms not in cryptography package are needed
RSA/DSA/ECC key generation and signing without OpenSSL dependency
avoid_when: New projects — prefer cryptography (PyCA) for better hazmat separation
When PyNaCl covers the use case (NaCl primitives are safer)
summary: PyCryptodome is a self-contained cryptographic library and PyCrypto drop-in replacement. AES.new(key, AES.MODE_GCM).encrypt_and_digest(data). RSA.generate(2048). SHA256/SHA3. AES-GCM, ChaCha20-Poly1305, RSA, ECC, PBKDF2, Scrypt. BSD-2 license. pycryptodomex for Cryptodome namespace. Use for PyCrypto migration or algorithms missing from cryptography package. For new code, prefer the cryptography (PyCA) package.
